name: ButterCMS Headless CMS API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly headless CMS with REST and GraphQL API — enabling development teams to add blog, pages, and content management to existing applications without building a CMS from scratch, with structured content types, a non-technical editor UI, webhooks for content events, and SDKs for all major frameworks (React, Vue, Next.js, Ruby, Python, PHP, iOS, Android).
use_cases: Adding blog and marketing page CMS capabilities to existing apps via ButterCMS API
Delivering blog content and landing pages to Next.js or Nuxt frontends via ButterCMS REST API
Managing structured content types (case studies, team pages, FAQs) via ButterCMS API
Triggering static site rebuilds on content publish via ButterCMS webhooks
Integrating ButterCMS with marketing tools via webhook for CRM and analytics sync on publish
not_for: Full enterprise DXP with marketing automation (use Kentico, Sitecore, or Salesforce CMS)
Complex permission hierarchies and multi-tenant publishing (use Contentful or Storyblok)
Self-hosted CMS (ButterCMS is SaaS-only with no self-hosting option)
best_when: A development team building a web application needs to add marketing content management (blog, landing pages, team pages) without spinning up a full CMS — with non-technical editors managing content and developers building the frontend.
avoid_when: You need enterprise DXP features, complex content workflows, multi-region deployment, or are building a content-heavy media site requiring advanced CMS capabilities.

auth.notes: API key (read API key and write API key) from ButterCMS dashboard. Bearer token auth. Site-scoped access. Webhook for content publish, update, and delete events. REST API for content delivery and management. SDKs available for 10+ languages and frameworks (React, Vue, Next.js, Angular, Svelte, Ruby, Python, PHP, Java, Go, iOS Swift, Android Kotlin). Preview API key for draft content. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Micro $99/month (1 user); Starter $166/month (3 users); Professional $333/month (10 users); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Chicago company. Founded 2014. Raised $1.5M seed. Bootstrapped-focused growth. Developer-first CMS with SDK-first approach — SDKs for virtually every framework. 10,000+ developers using ButterCMS. Single codebase for all clients (not separate products per framework). Blog management, page types, and content collections are core feature set. Simple pricing model. Competition from Contentful, Storyblok, Sanity. Focused on developer experience over enterprise features. Used by Netflix, Slack, Salesforce for marketing content.

performance.rate_limits: 10,000 API calls per month (Micro); unlimited on higher tiers

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for content delivery and management. API key auth (read vs. write keys). Excellent developer documentation with SDK examples for 10+ frameworks. Webhook for content events. Rate limit 10,000/month on Micro — very restrictive for high-traffic sites. Higher tiers have unlimited calls. Preview API for draft content. No OAuth2 — simple API key model.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Blog post reads idempotent by slug. Page reads idempotent by slug. Content creation uses slugs for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit 10,000 calls/month on Micro tier is very restrictive — upgrade for production use
Separate read and write API keys — use read key for delivery, write key for management only
US data residency only — EU customers need to evaluate GDPR adequacy
Preview API key gives access to draft content — protect from public exposure
No OAuth2 — API key provides full site access without scope granularity

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. US data residency. API key auth (read and write keys separate). Startup with SOC2 compliance. Read and write API key separation reduces risk of public key exposure. Write API key must be server-side only — never expose in client-side code. Webhook signatures for delivery verification. US data residency requires GDPR adequacy assessment for EU customers.
